topic: My fitness tracker logs a heart rate spike at exactly eleven forty seven PM every night while I am asleep
title: My Fitness Tracker Logs a Heart Rate Spike at 11:47 PM Every Night While I Sleep | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My fitness tracker logs a heart rate spike at eleven forty seven PM every night. I am asleep.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an Apple Watch on a wrist displaying a heart rate reading with a sudden spike in the graph, dim bedroom light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Apple Watch. Worn every night for three years. Same wrist.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of an Apple Watch on a bedside table charging, the screen showing the time, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I am asleep by ten thirty. Always.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a darkened bedroom with a bedside clock showing ten thirty PM, sheets pulled back, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Noticed it in April. Heart rate jumped to one fifty two. Then back to sixty.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone screen showing a heart rate chart with a sharp spike at eleven forty seven PM, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Thought it was a glitch. It was not.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a phone health app screen with a graph annotation marked GLITCH crossed out by hand, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Logged the same spike at eleven forty seven for sixty three nights.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a calendar with sixty three days marked with red X marks running down the page, vertical 9:16 framing
close shot of a hand resting on a comforter at night with an Apple Watch face visible, the wearer's pulse line steady, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Eleven forty seven. The spike registered. My heart did not change.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of an Apple Watch face displaying a heart rate of sixty BPM against a graph showing a spike to one fifty two, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Doctor had me wear a hospital monitor for a week.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a hospital cardiology room with a Holter monitor on a desk, leads coiled neatly, dim exam room light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Hospital monitor showed the same spike at the same time. My actual pulse stayed at sixty.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a printed Holter monitor report showing a nightly heart rate spike at eleven forty seven PM across multiple nights, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I have not worn the watch in two months.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an Apple Watch in a drawer with the band slack, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Whoever is wearing it is still spiking. Same time. Different body.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a health app on a phone screen showing the heart rate graph continuing to spike nightly, vertical 9:16 framing
